description: Tasks A2 + A4: Refactor TargetRegistry.resolve() to use detection pipeline, and create GovernanceProfile with Target/Profile separation. A2 depends on A1 (TargetDetector, DetectedTarget, TargetPinFile, JvmTargetDetector). A4 depends on A1 (TargetId). Both modify existing files.
steps: Step 1 - Create GovernanceProfile.java at kernel/src/main/java/com/bear/kernel/target/GovernanceProfile.java as a record with fields: TargetId target, String profileId. Factory method: static GovernanceProfile of(TargetId target, String profileId). Override toString() to return 'target.value()/profileId' format (e.g. 'jvm/backend-service'). Document known future profiles as comments: jvm/backend-service, node/backend-service, python/service, python/service-relaxed, react/feature-ui.

Step 2 - Add default method defaultProfile() to Target.java interface at kernel/src/main/java/com/bear/kernel/target/Target.java: 'default GovernanceProfile defaultProfile() { return GovernanceProfile.of(targetId(), "default"); }'. This is a default method so JvmTarget and any other implementations continue to compile without changes initially.

Step 3 - Override defaultProfile() in JvmTarget at kernel/src/main/java/com/bear/kernel/target/jvm/JvmTarget.java to return GovernanceProfile.of(TargetId.JVM, 'backend-service'). Add the @Override method near the top of the class, after the targetId() method.

Step 4 - Create TargetResolutionException.java at kernel/src/main/java/com/bear/kernel/target/TargetResolutionException.java. Fields: String code (TARGET_NOT_DETECTED, TARGET_AMBIGUOUS, TARGET_PIN_INVALID), String path, String remediation. Extends RuntimeException. Constructor takes code, path, remediation, and calls super with a formatted message.

Step 5 - Modify TargetRegistry.java at kernel/src/main/java/com/bear/kernel/target/TargetRegistry.java: (a) Add a field 'private final List<TargetDetector> detectors'. (b) Add a new constructor that accepts both Map<TargetId, Target> and List<TargetDetector>. (c) Keep the existing single-arg constructor for backward compatibility -- it should create an empty detector list. (d) Refactor resolve(Path projectRoot) to implement detection pipeline: first check TargetPinFile.read(projectRoot.resolve('.bear')), if pin present look up in targets map (throw TargetResolutionException if not registered), if pin absent run all detectors and collect results. Exactly one SUPPORTED = return that target. Zero SUPPORTED = throw TargetResolutionException(TARGET_NOT_DETECTED). Multiple SUPPORTED = throw TargetResolutionException(TARGET_AMBIGUOUS). If any UNSUPPORTED result shares the same targetId as a SUPPORTED result, block that resolution. Unrelated UNSUPPORTED results are silently ignored. (e) Update defaultRegistry() to include JvmTargetDetector in the detector list. (f) Important: the DEFAULT static field must be updated to use the new constructor with detectors. (g) Remove the constraint that JVM target must always be present -- the registry should work with any set of targets+detectors.

Step 6 - Create unit test GovernanceProfileTest.java at kernel/src/test/java/com/bear/kernel/GovernanceProfileTest.java. Tests: construction and equality; toString() format 'jvm/backend-service'; JvmTarget.defaultProfile() returns jvm/backend-service; different profiles for same target are not equal; Target.defaultProfile() default returns target/default for unoverridden implementations (create a simple stub implementing Target interface for this test).

Step 7 - Create unit test TargetRegistryResolveTest.java at kernel/src/test/java/com/bear/kernel/TargetRegistryResolveTest.java. Tests with @TempDir: (a) single JVM project with build.gradle resolves JvmTarget; (b) pin file override: .bear/target.id='jvm' skips detectors; (c) no match throws TargetResolutionException with TARGET_NOT_DETECTED; (d) ambiguous (multiple SUPPORTED) throws TARGET_AMBIGUOUS; (e) invalid pin file throws exception; (f) unrelated UNSUPPORTED does not block valid SUPPORTED resolution.

Step 8 - Verify existing TargetRegistryTest.java at kernel/src/test/java/com/bear/kernel/TargetRegistryTest.java still compiles and is semantically correct after TargetRegistry changes. The existing tests use the single-arg Map constructor and call resolve(Path.of('.')) -- since the single-arg constructor creates empty detectors and the existing resolve with empty detectors should fall back to JVM target (backward compatibility), these tests must pass. If backward compatibility requires adjustment, handle it.
acceptance_criteria: GovernanceProfile record compiles with correct toString() format
Target.java has defaultProfile() default method that does not break existing implementations
TargetResolutionException has code, path, remediation fields
TargetRegistry accepts detectors in constructor alongside target map
TargetRegistry.resolve() implements full detection pipeline: pin check, detector run, SUPPORTED/UNSUPPORTED handling
Existing single-arg TargetRegistry constructor still works for backward compatibility
Existing TargetRegistryTest.java compiles and semantics are preserved
All new test files compile with JUnit 5 patterns
verification: Compile all new and modified source files with javac 17
Compile existing TargetRegistryTest.java to verify backward compatibility
